mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equities and eases financial conditions, so sustained declines in policy rates should precede positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not higher after FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS in at least 60% of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS stays above 3%, real-rate pressure rises and equity multiples compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above 3% while FEDFUNDS > 3%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day returns more often than not, or if the conditional mean return is not below unconditional average by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, so an upward labor-market shock should predict lower S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE increases fail to precede below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns across rolling out-of-sample windows, or if the sign flips positive for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden deterioration in PAYEMS combined with a higher UNRATE marks labor shedding and income risk, which should depress equities over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change falls below 0 and UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp in 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if episodes meeting the joint labor trigger do not have lower 20-day forward returns than non-trigger episodes, or if the interaction term is insignificant in all 3-year rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO indicates softer industrial activity and weaker cyclical earnings momentum, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if falling INDPRO is not associated with below-average 20-day returns in a majority of walk-forward samples, or if the sign remains positive through a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in RSAFS signals weaker retail demand and softer nominal sales growth, which should lead S&P 500 returns lower over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 2-month change is negative and below its 12-month median change
failure_condition: Reject if negative RSAFS changes do not correspond to negative or sub-median 20-day equity returns, or if the hit rate falls below 55% over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls while GDPC1 growth is already slowing, the housing-led demand impulse weakens and equities should underperform over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth is below 2% and HOUST declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined housing-growth trigger does not predict lower 60-day returns than either proxy alone, or if conditional returns are not negative in the majority of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-inflation peak regime, stable CPIAUCSL with no further increase in FEDFUNDS supports multiple expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if weekly S&P 500 returns are not positive when CPIAUCSL is flat-to-falling and FEDFUNDS is unchanged, or if the signal fails to outperform baseline over any 18-month rolling window.
